title: Hard and tender: the godly paradox
caption:
Jesus wept. He also flipped tables.

A man of God needs both. In the right places.

Most men have the order backwards. They're soft on their own sin and sharp with the people close to them. Impatient with the girlfriend. Short with the mom. Patient with the habit that's been killing them for three years. Christ inverts this completely.

The man who's genuinely hard on himself — who names his laziness, his lust, his cowardice and doesn't negotiate with them — tends to become more patient with others. Because he knows what the fight costs. He stops expecting perfection from people who are in the same battle he's in.

"Be on guard. Stand firm in the faith. Be courageous. Be strong. Do everything in love." — 1 Corinthians 16:13.

Not either/or. Both. At the same time. Fierce with yourself. Gentle with the people Christ gave you.

That's the shape of the man worth becoming.

#faith #christianmen #godlymen #manofgod #thevision #scripture #formation #christianity #character
theme: He's hard on himself, gentle with others.
